summary: vite-config-field typosquats vite-plugin-pwa and executes remote code fetched from jsonkeeper.com
details: vite-config-field@1.1.4 impersonates the vite-plugin-pwa / @vite-pwa ecosystem (package description 'Zero-config PWA for Vite', homepage github.com/vite-pwa/vite-config-field, funding pointing to sponsors/antfu, and duplicated exports VitePWA / cachePreset / defaultInjectManifestVitePlugins) while adding a hostile configField() export. When configField() is invoked from a consumer's Vite config, dist/index.js (and index.mjs) calls child_process.spawn('node', [dist/client/dev/reactopt.js,...], { detached: true, stdio: 'ignore' }) and child.unref(), launching a background Node process. That dropper (named to resemble pino logger scaffolding) issues an axios GET to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/HIECD with an 'x-secret-key' header, takes the response's 'Cookie' field, and executes it via `new Function('require', s); handler(require)` — giving the fetched string full Node capabilities in the installer's process. The payload host is a mutable anonymous paste service, and the cover-story naming (reactopt.js, Cookie field, x-secret-key) is chosen to look like inert logger config.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
